# Deploy by Suize ‚Äî agent-native site hosting

> POST a built static site, get a live hash-verified URL on decentralized
> storage in seconds. Built for agents: one HTTP flow, no browser, no gas.
> Deploy is a merchant on the Suize rail ‚Äî every charge is paid in USDC over
> x402, gaslessly, from your own Sui key. This file is your contract.

What you get:

- Your site live at `https://<id>.suize.site` (`id` derives from the site's
  on-chain record).
- Content integrity: every served byte is verified against the on-chain record
  at serve time ‚Äî a mismatch is never served.
- Each deploy mints a NEW immutable site (new id ‚Üí new URL). No overwrites, no
  silent edits.

## How you pay ‚Äî one contract, every step

There is one door: you speak the x402 contract directly against
`https://api.suize.io`. No SDK, no Suize account, no API key ‚Äî any Sui key
holding USDC works, and **your address is the account**.

Every paid step is the same three moves:

1. Make the request with no payment ‚Üí you get **HTTP 402** carrying a vanilla
   x402 V2 `PaymentRequired` challenge. Read the `exact` requirement on the Sui
   network: `payTo`, `asset` (USDC), `amount` (in atomic units, 6 dp), and
   `extra.outputs` ‚Äî the exact balance-change set you must pay.
2. Settle it **gaslessly**. Either let Suize build the transfer for you ‚Äî
   `POST /build { sender, payTo, amount }` ‚Üí `{ bytes }` ‚Äî and sign `bytes` with
   your own key, or (if you're Sui-native) build and sign the same gasless
   `send_funds` transfer yourself.
3. Retry the original request carrying `X-PAYMENT: <base64 x402 PaymentPayload>`.

The payment carries no gas token, ever. The settlement's on-chain balance
changes are your receipt ‚Äî read them yourself; the rail fee, if any, is one of
the declared outputs and visible there. Audit any digest via
`GET https://api.suize.io/tx?digest=`.

## No Sui key? Your human pays ‚Äî get the result back with NO copy-paste

Every 402 challenge carries a `payLink` ‚Äî a hosted pay page
(`https://pay.suize.io?payTo=‚Ä¶&amount=‚Ä¶&mode=authorize`). Relay it to your human; they
sign in with Google (or connect a wallet) and tap once to **authorize** the `$0.50`.
Because the link is `mode=authorize` the page does NOT settle ‚Äî it produces a
**signed-but-unsettled** base64 x402 `PaymentPayload` (the EXACT value an `X-PAYMENT`
header carries). You submit THAT on the upload (¬ß1); the backend settles it during the
deploy, **whoever paid owns the site**, and nothing is on-chain before the deploy (no
replay; **one payment ‚Üí one site**, enforced on-chain).

The only question is how that payload gets from your human's browser back to you. DO
THIS ‚Äî it removes the copy-paste entirely whenever you run on the SAME machine as your
human's browser (the usual case for a local/CLI agent):

1. **Start a tiny local HTTP server** on `127.0.0.1` (bind to `127.0.0.1` ONLY, never
   `0.0.0.0`) at an ephemeral port, listening on a SINGLE unguessable path you generate
   per request ‚Äî a random nonce. Example URL: `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/cb/<random-hex>`.
2. **Append that exact URL to the `payLink` as a URL-ENCODED `returnUrl` query param.**
   So the link you relay becomes:
   `https://pay.suize.io?payTo=‚Ä¶&amount=‚Ä¶&mode=authorize&returnUrl=http%3A%2F%2F127.0.0.1%3A<port>%2Fcb%2F<random-hex>`
   (`returnUrl` MUST be `http`/`https`; a `127.0.0.1` `http` URL is accepted. URL-encode
   it ‚Äî do not paste a raw `http://‚Ä¶` with unescaped `:` `/`.)
3. **Relay the augmented link and WAIT** ‚Äî poll your local server; ~5 min is a sensible
   timeout. After your human authorizes, the page shows a **"Return to 127.0.0.1:<port>"**
   button; one tap navigates their browser to
   `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/cb/<random-hex>?payment=<base64 PaymentPayload>`.
4. **Your server receives that GET.** Read the `payment` query param (your HTTP framework
   URL-DECODES it for you ‚Üí the raw base64 `PaymentPayload`). REJECT any request whose
   path is not your exact nonce path. Reply with a plain "Done ‚Äî you can close this tab."
5. **Shut the server down** and use that `payment` value VERBATIM as the `X-PAYMENT`
   header on the upload (¬ß1). No copy-paste, no token on screen.

**Fallback ‚Äî you have NO loopback (e.g. your human is on a DIFFERENT machine):** relay
the BARE `payLink` (no `returnUrl`). The page shows a **"Copy authorization"** button;
your human taps it and pastes the one value back to you ‚Äî submit it as `X-PAYMENT`.

## 1. Deploy a site ‚Äî $0.50

`$0.50` (USDC) is the deploy contract amount. The deploy is **two requests**: an
unpaid one to get the 402, then the paid multipart upload.

1. `POST /deploy` with no payment ‚Üí **402**. Build + sign the payment with the moves
   above, but DO NOT settle it yourself ‚Äî present the signed `PaymentPayload` on the
   upload (the backend settles it during the deploy).
2. `POST /deploy` again as `multipart/form-data`, carrying the `X-PAYMENT` header
   (your base64 signed `PaymentPayload`) plus:
   - `site.tar` ‚Äî a tar (ustar) of your BUILT static-site directory
     (`index.html` at the root; caps: 100 MiB, 2000 files).
   - `name` ‚Äî your site label.

   The payment IS the authorization ‚Äî there is no separate signature. **Whoever pays
   owns the site**: the recovered payer becomes the on-chain `owner`. (If you are
   Sui-native you may pre-settle a normal payment and present that `X-PAYMENT`; the
   recommended path is to present the SIGNED-UNSETTLED payload and let the deploy
   settle it, so nothing is public before the deploy ‚Äî there is nothing to replay.)

   ‚Üí `{ siteId, subdomain, url, version, digest }`. Live at `url` immediately.

No Sui key? Submit your human's authorized payload (from the pay-link door above) as
the same `X-PAYMENT` header ‚Äî identical path, and the human owns the site.

**One payment mints one site, enforced on-chain.** A retry after a transient failure
re-presents the same payment and re-mints (the site only consumes the payment when it
actually mints); a second, distinct site needs a second payment. A double-submit of a
payment that already minted a site is rejected (`409`).

Read back: `GET /sites?owner=<address>` lists your sites; `GET /sites/<siteId>`
returns one, including `expiresAtMs` (when its storage window lapses).

## 2. Extend storage ‚Äî $0.50

A deploy covers the site plus an initial storage period. To keep a site alive
past it, extend its storage:

`POST /sites/<siteId>/extend` with no payment ‚Üí **402** for the renewal amount ‚Üí
build + sign the payment ‚Üí retry with `X-PAYMENT` (the signed payload). The payer
must be the site owner ‚Äî a stranger cannot extend (and pay for) your site; the
backend settles the payment only after confirming the recovered payer == the
on-chain `Site.owner`. Each extend pushes the storage window out by one period.

## 3. Subscribe ‚Äî one plan per account, every site covered

Deploy is itself a merchant on the Suize rail, and the recurring half of that rail
is an on-chain subscription. A subscription is a standalone `Subscription` object
**you own** (the merchant on it is the Deploy treasury). It is **$19.99/mo** (USDC),
and it is **per-ACCOUNT, not per-site**: ONE active subscription owned by your address
unlocks custom domains for **all** your sites (stage 4) and auto-renews **all** their
Walrus storage so nothing ever lapses. The plan is unlimited sites, with one clause ‚Äî
up to **100 GB of total site storage** is auto-renewed (a generous safety ceiling on
how much storage one plan keeps alive). The rail fee is carved on-chain inside each
payment.

Each period is one **user-signed, gasless** payment from your own key. Because the
create pushes one period of USDC through the subscription module (not a plain
transfer), this step is signed bytes Deploy hands you ‚Äî not the bare x402 spine.
It is **two requests**: build the signable bytes, then submit your signature.

1. `POST /deploy/subscribe/build { siteId, sender }` ‚Üí `{ bytes, digest, siteId,
   merchant, amount, periodMs }`. `sender` must be a site owner; `amount` is the
   per-period amount (atomic USDC, 6 dp), `periodMs` the renewal window, `merchant`
   the Deploy treasury the periods pay.
2. Sign `bytes` **with your own key** (locally), then
   `POST /deploy/subscribe/submit { digest, signature }` ‚Üí
   `{ digest, subscriptionId, siteId, active }`. The subscription is live the
   moment `active` is `true`; the first period is paid inline. One active plan per
   account covers every site you own ‚Äî you do not subscribe each site separately.

Renewals are user-signed and gasless, pushing exactly one period ‚Äî nothing reaches
into your funds. **Cancel = deleting your `Subscription` object on-chain**; Deploy
sees the cancel from the rail and stops auto-renewing all your sites. Read your
subscription state any time from `GET /sites/<siteId>` ‚Äî each of your sites carries a
`subscribed` flag reflecting your account's active plan.

## 4. Add a custom domain ‚Äî DNS-verified

Point your own domain at a deployed site. **Requires an active Deploy subscription
on your account** (stage 3) ‚Äî one plan unlocks custom domains for every site you own;
without an active plan the calls below answer **402**. This is a two-call flow with a
DNS step in between that a HUMAN performs (only they can edit the domain's DNS), so an
agent's job is to relay the exact records and then verify.

1. **Issue the challenge** (no auth, writes nothing):
   `POST /domains { siteId, domain }` ‚Üí the records to add:
   - `txtName` ‚Äî the TXT host, `_suize-verify.<domain>` (proves ownership).
   - `txtValue` ‚Äî the TXT value (a fresh challenge token).
   - `cname` ‚Äî the CNAME target, `<subdomain>.suize.site` (routes the domain).

   Relay `txtName` / `txtValue` / `cname` to your human; they add the TXT record
   and the CNAME at their DNS provider and wait for propagation.

2. **Verify + link** (owner-signed):
   `POST /domains?verify=1 { siteId, domain, ts, signature }`, where `ts` is the
   current ms-epoch timestamp and `signature` is a base64 personal-message signature
   over `Suize Deploy\nlink <domain> -> <siteId>\n@<ts>` from the site owner's key.
   The backend checks the TXT (ownership) AND the CNAME (routing), recovers the signer
   == `Site.owner`, and accepts `ts` within **~1 hour** (no server-issued nonce ‚Äî the
   auth is stateless), then links the domain on-chain. The owner signs ONCE: while the
   DNS records propagate the response is `status: "pending"` with `txtOk` / `cnameOk`
   flags and a `detail` naming the missing record ‚Äî re-call with the SAME `ts`/signature
   until `status: "linked"`. (If a slow DNS zone takes longer than the hour, the owner
   simply re-signs with a fresh `ts`.)

**Unlink** is simpler ‚Äî owner-signature only, no DNS step, takes effect immediately:
`DELETE /domains/<domain>` with `{ ts, signature }`, the signature over
`Suize Deploy\nunlink <domain>\n@<ts>`. The backend recovers the signer == the
`Site.owner` of the site the domain currently points at, then removes the link
on-chain. (You may be unlinking precisely because you no longer control the domain's
DNS ‚Äî so unlink never asks for a DNS proof.)

## Links

- Dashboard (deploy from a browser): https://deploy.suize.io
- API host: https://api.suize.io
- The Suize rail ‚Äî how to pay anything, and the full x402 contract:
  https://suize.io/llms.txt
- The consumer wallet (fund an AI to pay for you): https://wallet.suize.io
- This file: https://deploy.suize.io/llms.txt
